heaviest_rainfall.status: Record-breaking rain fell on 09 November 2025 with a total accumulation of 47.89mm over 144.8 hours and falling at a rate of 24.6mm per hour at times.

heaviest_rainrate.status: A torrential downpour occurred on 02 September 2025 when the rain reached a maximum intensity of 34.3mm per hour.  This happened during a 15.0 hour storm when 21.19mm of rain fell.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning strikes in one day since 11 April 2025 is 225 and it happened on 28 May 2026.

storm.status: No storms are expected.  The last storm was observed on 18 July 2026 and continued for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: A significant dry period is on-going and is ranked 2nd for length since 23 September 2024.  So far, it has lasted 18 consecutive days beginning 03 July 2026.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: At the moment, it is not a cold snap.  This year has seen 3 cold snaps; typically, 3.7 cold snaps happen every year.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 27.7°C, observed on 24 April 2026 when the temperature fell to -4.5°C but also managed to reach 23.2°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: There is currently a heatwave.  It has been going on for 20 days and it started on 2026-07-01.  Currently this year there have been 3 heatwaves, usually there are 1.9 heatwaves per year.

tropical.status: The most recent tropical night was on 28 June 2026 and this has only occurred 68 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (209°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 128.2 mph from 27 January 2026.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The record wind speed remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 3.0°C) and outside temperature (down 2.6°C).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 8.6°C), feels like temperature (up 8.2°C), outside temperature (up 5.2°C), and outside humidity (up 13%) when compared to today.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures should be quite comfortable. Outside; the temperature will feel quite hot. Also, humidity should not be a concern and calm and still conditions are expected. Chance of light rain, mostly clear skies with high cloud, and bright sunshine will dominate the day. No significant shifts in the weather are expected.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
